explanation: Conversation topics have drifted from the stated objective
conversation_text: Hey B, I was reading this thing about temperance the other day. You know, like one of those old virtues? I think it's super underrated. What do you think about it? Temperance? Uh, yeah, I've heard of it. Isn't that just like not overdoing stuff? Like, moderation in eating, drinking, all that? Why'd you bring it up? Exactly! But it's way more than that. Temperance is the absolute key to a perfect life. I mean, if everyone practiced it 100%, there'd be no wars, no obesity epidemics, nothing. It's foolproof. Whoa, hold on. Perfect life? That seems kinda extreme. Like, sure, not bingeing on pizza every night is good, but wars? Come on, that's not just about self-control. No, no, hear me out. Wars happen because leaders lack temperance—they get greedy or angry. And obesity? Pure lack of temperance in eating. It's all connected. Science backs it up too; studies show moderation leads to longer life, happier people. Undeniable.
